name: ALT Erogenous
classification_notes: A high-contrast display serif with pronounced stroke variation and decorative serifs, designed exclusively for large-scale headline and display use rather than continuous text.
summary: A single-weight display serif revived from the 1972 cover lettering of Ingrid Bengis's 'Combat in the Erogenous Zone', with pronounced stroke contrast and decorative curvature.
description: The letterforms trace back to the cover of Ingrid Bengis's 1972 book on female identity and emotional duality, which gives the revival a specific cultural anchor rather than a generic historical reference. High stroke contrast and sensually curved serifs create visual tension suited to posters, editorial headlines and branding. At 484 glyphs it covers 219 Latin languages, including Full Latin 1 and Extended-A.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe ALT Erogenous as a bold display serif balancing sharp contrast with seductive curvature, revived from the cover lettering of Ingrid Bengis's 1972 book 'Combat in the Erogenous Zone'. They frame it as a typographic response to the emotional and social complexities explored in that text, visualising internal tension and cultural questioning through form. The family supports 219 Latin languages across 484 glyphs.

name: ALT Gumbo
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes ALT Gumbo as a high-contrast neo-grotesque, with the even stroke modulation and rational construction characteristic of that sub-genre.
summary: A high-contrast neo-grotesque with seven weights, short ascenders and descenders, and coverage across Latin Extended and Basic Cyrillic.
description: Built for snug line-height settings, ALT Gumbo pairs its high stroke contrast with short ascenders and descenders to hold together tightly set text in both digital and print contexts. The family spans seven weights from Thin to Black, and ships with a solid OpenType feature set including stylistic alternates, old-style figures, case-sensitive punctuation and fractions. Latin Extended A coverage is complete, and Basic Cyrillic is included, making it a workable choice for multilingual editorial and identity work.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe ALT Gumbo as a high-contrast neo-grotesque suited to contemporary digital and printed typesetting. They highlight its short ascenders and descenders as making it particularly effective at snug line-height settings, and note a feature set covering stylistic alternates, old-style figures, superiors, inferiors and fractions across 850-plus glyphs, with Full Latin and Basic Cyrillic support.

name: ALT Junction
classification_notes: ALT Junction draws from retro geometric display faces such as Motter Tektura, Stop and Quickstep, placing it firmly in the display category with strong geometric and decorative characteristics rather than a functional text role.
summary: A single-weight display face from ALT.tf drawing on retro geometric references including Motter Tektura, Stop and Quickstep.
description: Rather than reviving any one historical source, ALT Junction collages influences from late-20th-century geometric display types, pairing that retro backbone with a contemporary digital sensibility. The single Bold weight covers 468 glyphs with full Latin-A support, stylistic alternates, and directional arrow symbols. It suits poster, identity and branding work that wants a confident, unconventional character without nostalgia for its own sake.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe ALT Junction as a collage of ideas and explorations drawing inspiration from historical masterworks including Motter Tektura (Othmar Motter, 1974), Stop (Aldo Novarese, 1970–71) and Quickstep (Claes Nordenstam, 1976), while not attempting to revive any of them. They characterise it as having a retro backbone and a digital personality, and note it is not a wallflower by any chance.

name: ALT Klarinet
classification_notes: A contemporary stencil typeface built on a sans-serif skeleton with clean geometric lines and deliberate stencil cuts, placing it in the display category with a stencil sans subcategory.
summary: A contemporary stencil sans from London-based ALT.tf, running six weights from Thin to Bold across a broad Latin character set.
description: Built on clean, geometric lines, the stencil cuts are consistent across all six weights, maintaining legibility at smaller sizes as well as at display scale. With over 393 glyphs and support for 244 languages, the family covers an unusually wide range of Latin diacritics for a display typeface. Work in the wild includes wine packaging, brand identity and editorial projects.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe ALT Klarinet as their first contemporary sans-serif stencil typeface, blending modern design with classic stencil details. Six weights from Thin to Bold offer flexibility, with strong, clean lines intended to create visual impact at any scale. The family includes 393+ glyphs, diacritics, symbols, fractions and support for 244+ languages.

name: ALT Maria Clara
classification_notes: A refined display serif with sharp, intricate letterforms drawing on Philippine visual history; its high contrast and ornamental detailing position it firmly in the display category rather than text use.
summary: A sharp, high-contrast display serif by Alli Cunanan, designed as an act of cultural reclamation rooted in Philippine visual history.
description: Designed by Alli Cunanan and named after the colonial Filipina archetype Maria Clara, the typeface inverts its source symbol by channelling confidence and intricacy rather than submissive restraint. Five weights from Light to Bold carry 592 glyphs with stylistic alternates, oldstyle figures and ligatures across full Latin Extended-A coverage. The result is a striking display face suited to editorial, branding and identity work where visual weight and cultural specificity matter.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe ALT Maria Clara as a font dedicated to strong, confident, brave Filipina women breaking away from stereotypes. Named after a fictional Filipino character who became a colonial symbol of submissive demureness, the typeface flips that narrative by drawing on the visual history of the Philippines through sharp, intricate details crafted by designer Alli Cunanan.

name: ALT Nadrey
classification_notes: Nadrey features soft, rounded letterforms with subtle serifs and smooth transitions, placing it within the humanist serif tradition. Its organic, calligraphic quality sets it apart from more geometric or transitional serif styles.
summary: A single-weight humanist serif by Côte d'Ivoire-based designer O'Plérou Grebet, distinguished by soft curves and rounded letterforms drawn from personal and cultural affection.
description: Designed by O'Plérou Grebet, known for developing visual language tools rooted in African culture, Nadrey takes its name from the Bété word for 'My Heart'. The typeface carries rounded, organic letterforms with gentle serifs and smooth stroke transitions, lending it a warm and expressive character suited to editorial, branding and cookbook design. It covers 98% of Latin languages through an extended character set of over 300 glyphs including diacritics, fractions and symbols.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe Nadrey as a typographical rendition of love, nostalgia and self-expression by Côte d'Ivoire-based designer O'Plérou Grebet. The typeface features soft serifs, rounded letterforms and smooth transitions that convey, in the designer's words, 'a natural, organic energy' and 'sophisticated softness'. It supports 98% of Latin languages globally and includes over 300 glyphs.

name: ALT Picaro
classification_notes: Clean lines, uniform stroke weights and subtly rounded terminals point clearly to a geometric sans construction. The wide stance and elevated crossbar are characteristic geometric traits with a slight humanist warmth introduced through the terminal treatment.
summary: A single-weight geometric sans with uniform stroke weights, subtly rounded terminals and broad Latin language support across 484 glyphs.
description: Built around clean geometry and a wide stance, it pairs an elevated crossbar with gently rounded terminals to soften what is otherwise a crisp, precise design. The sole Regular weight covers 484 glyphs, full Latin Extended-A and 95% of Latin Plus languages, making it a practical choice for international brand or editorial work. Its character sits somewhere between a neutral display grotesque and a warmer, personality-driven face, suited to titles and short-form digital copy.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe ALT Picaro as a bold, modern typeface that blends geometric structure with a touch of playfulness, designed for maximum clarity and character. They highlight its clean lines, uniform stroke weights and subtly rounded terminals, noting its wide stance and elevated crossbar give it strong presence in titles while remaining legible at smaller sizes. With 484 glyphs and support for 282 languages, they position it for brand identities, digital products and editorial layouts.

name: ALT Rigatoni
classification_notes: A wide, extended grotesque with brutalist uppercase character and a looser, friendlier lowercase, placing it firmly in the grotesque sans tradition with display-oriented proportions.
summary: A wide extended grotesque in four weights that pairs a stark brutalist uppercase with a looser, more approachable lowercase.
description: Designed for posters and web use, the face carries 468 glyphs across Light, Regular, Semibold and Bold, with full Latin-A coverage and multilingual support. An alternate set of thin symbols and punctuation offsets the heavy weight of the main characters, adding visual contrast within a single setting. Stylistic alternates, standard and discretionary ligatures, fractions and a full arrow set round out the feature list.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe Rigatoni as a wide, extended Grotesk designed to create a brutalist look in all caps, met with a quirky and mellow feel in the lowercase. Intended as flexible enough for both posters and web pages, the heavy character weight is balanced by an alternate set of thin symbols and punctuation, with the loose design lending an overall friendly feel.

name: ALT Riviera
classification_notes: The foundry describes it as a contemporary grotesk that borrows from geometric logic without being fully geometric; its construction and character decisions place it firmly in the grotesque tradition with humanist inflections.
summary: A London-made grotesque with six weights, italics and a monospaced cut, where deliberate tension in the letterforms keeps it from collapsing into anonymity.
description: Built on geometric logic but refusing to be rigidly sanitised, ALT Riviera introduces subtle irregularities in curves and joins that give the family a distinct character without veering into gimmickry. The R and G carry particular personality, and the proportions resist pure neutrality across both display and text settings. Over 600 glyphs cover Latin Extended A, partial Latin Extended B and Greek, with full italic and monospaced accompaniments across all six weights.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe Riviera as a contemporary grotesk that sits in the modern sans-serif scene — clean, functional and usable — but resists anonymity the closer you look. Curves are occasionally slightly off, joins feel considered, and certain letters carry tension points that give the font a pulse. The balance between control and looseness, soft and sharp, is what makes it work. It comes with six weights, matching italics and a monospaced cut, suitable for both display and text use.

name: ALT Riviera Mono
classification_notes: A fixed-width typeface built on the geometric logic of the proportional Riviera family, with consistent stroke weight and grotesque letterforms characteristic of monospaced grotesques.
summary: A fixed-width companion to the Riviera family that preserves the proportional cuts' geometric character and subtle irregularities within strict monospaced constraints.
description: Built on the same geometric logic as the proportional Riviera styles, this mono resists the sterile quality common to fixed-width type by retaining slight asymmetric curves and intentional joins. Six weights run from ExtraLight to ExtraBold, each with italics, covering over 600 glyphs including Latin Extended A, partial Latin Extended B and Greek. Suited to code, data and interface work, though its controlled personality keeps it usable in editorial contexts too.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe Riviera Mono as distilling the character of the wider Riviera family into a fixed-width system without sanding off its personality. They position it as a contemporary monospaced grotesk prioritising clarity and rhythm while retaining subtle irregularities — curves hold their slight asymmetries, joins stay intentional, and tension points persist through the letterforms, giving it what they call a steady pulse. Designed for code, data, interfaces and tables, but intended to hold up in non-technical contexts too.

name: ALT Systema
classification_notes: Clean, contemporary sans-serif with geometric proportions and a neutral, professional character across seven weights. The even stroke contrast and upright construction place it firmly in the geometric sans tradition.
summary: A clean geometric sans by Giulia Boggio in seven weights, covering 95% of Latin-based languages with over 400 glyphs.
description: Built with a consistent, upright construction and even stroke weight, it sits comfortably across branding, editorial and digital work where clarity is the priority. Seven weights from Thin to Black give reasonable range without italics, and the broad Latin language coverage makes it practical for multilingual projects. In-use examples from cosmetics packaging to architecture identities suggest it finds most use in straightforward professional contexts.
foundry_blurb: ALT.tf describe ALT Systema as a contemporary sans-serif built to provide a professional touch with clean design that ensures clarity across both digital and printed media. Equipped with seven weights from Thin to Black, over 400 glyphs including fractions and basic symbols, and support for 95% of Latin-based languages, the family is designed to handle a wide range of typographic needs.
